title: Doctest fixes (4): RED → GREEN
head_note: M2 fixes applied on top of M1 backfill commit d53c638 (which sits on M1 main commit fde7c5f). The pre-commit HEAD is recorded above; the M2 commit_sha will be backfilled in a follow-up chore commit (same pattern as M1 → d53c638).

build_status.interpretation: Clean build after the 4 doctest source edits. No regression from M1 GREEN baseline.

implemented: Fix 1 — crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/cross_file_types.rs L1025-L1037: REWROTE the FuncIndexProxy doctest example to use FuncIndexProxyMut (the working impl at L1147) instead of FuncIndexProxy (the unimplemented! stub at L1109). Added a 3-line note explaining that FuncIndexProxy::insert is currently unimplemented and FuncIndexProxyMut should be used during construction. RATIONALE: per no-gaming rule, provide a working example that actually demonstrates the type's intended use; do not lie about the stub being callable.
Fix 2 — crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/languages/kotlin.rs L74: Changed bare ``` fence to ```text. Pseudo-grammar block now renders as preformatted text instead of being compiled as Rust source.
Fix 3 — crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/languages/luau.rs L217: Same edit as kotlin: bare ``` → ```text. Pseudo-grammar block now renders as preformatted text.

Fix 4 — crates/tldr-core/src/surface/triggers.rs L16: Stale import path `tldr_core::contracts::triggers::extract_name_triggers` corrected to `tldr_core::surface::triggers::extract_name_triggers`. The function lives in `surface/`, not `contracts/`. Confirmed by reading the function definition at L22 in the same file (it IS in `surface/triggers`).
reports/M2-doctest-capture.txt — cargo test --workspace --features semantic --doc --no-fail-fast capture confirming 0 failed doctests across all 4 crates.

modified: crates/tldr-core/src/surface/triggers.rs (doctest import path at L16 — contracts → surface)
tests.rationale: M2 is purely a doctest fix milestone. RED was captured in M1 (reports/M1-doctest-red-capture.txt). GREEN captured here in reports/M2-doctest-capture.txt.

results_summary: tldr-cli: 0 passed; 0 failed; 6 ignored (unchanged from M1)
tldr-core: 33 passed; 0 failed; 147 ignored (was 31 passed; 4 failed; 147 ignored at M1)
doctest_zero_failed.delta_total: -2 (182→180); 2 doctests were converted to ```text fenced blocks (kotlin/luau) so they no longer execute as doctests — expected and intentional

loc_delta_note: Target was ~15 LOC (3 one-line edits + 1 doctest rewrite). Actual is 18 LOC — the cross_file_types rewrite added a 3-line clarifying note about FuncIndexProxy vs FuncIndexProxyMut (per no-gaming rule, do not silently hide that ::insert is unimplemented). Within reasonable variance.
discoveries: FuncIndexProxyMut::get returns Option<&str> (not Option<&String>) — confirmed by reading the impl at L1179. The doctest uses Some("src/mymodule.py") which is the &'static str literal coercion, exactly matching the return type.

The kotlin/luau ```text conversion has a side-effect: those 2 doctests no longer EXECUTE; they only RENDER as preformatted text. This reduces the executed-doctest count from 31→33 (+2 from cross_file_types and surface::triggers becoming pass) but the total-tests count drops by 2 (the kotlin/luau text blocks). This is the correct fix — the blocks are pseudo-grammar, not Rust, and were never meant to compile.

The surface::triggers stale import path (tldr_core::contracts::...) is a copy-paste artifact from before a contracts→surface refactor; both modules exist in the crate but the function lives only in surface/. No contracts/ module was reintroduced; the import was simply pointing to the wrong path.

compliance.no_gaming_or_bypassing: FuncIndexProxy doctest was not silenced via #[doc(hidden)] or removed; instead rewritten to demonstrate the working alternative (FuncIndexProxyMut) and add an explanatory note about the unimplemented! stub. The kotlin/luau fences were not deleted; they were correctly tagged as text (which is what they always were). The triggers import was fixed to point to the real path, not stubbed.
compliance.cargo_lock_not_staged: Cargo.lock not modified by these edits and not staged.
compliance.explicit_add_only: Only the 4 source files + the 2 plan-dir artifacts (M2-doctest-capture.txt + M2-report.json) staged. No git add -A or git add . used.
compliance.no_sibling_milestone_files: Verified — only workspace-test-infrastructure-v1-plan/reports/ artifacts touched in continuum/.
compliance.no_push_no_publish_no_version_bump: Confirmed — no remote ops, no Cargo.toml version edits, no cargo publish.
compliance.regular_test_baseline_preserved: Doctest fixes are inside doc comments (text changes) for kotlin/luau; an import path inside a doc-comment example for triggers; and a doctest rewrite for cross_file_types. NONE of these alter executable code paths or function signatures, so unit/integration test outcomes are mathematically identical to M1 baseline. cargo build --workspace --tests --features semantic exit 0 confirms compilation.

next_milestone: M3 ATOMIC — bulk delete ~218-225 archived-subcommand tests per dispatch-contract §M3.
